channel.title: Posts on Kleinhaus
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Kleinhaus

item.title: CPU Virtualization II: Scheduling
item.description: Introduction In this post, we complete our exploration of cpu virtualization by looking at how the OS and the processor work together to allow user programs to effectively and fairly share the CPU. As mentioned in the previous article, this post is derived from the notes I made while reading the best OS book on the internet CPU Time-Sharing Whenever a program is being executed, the OS is not running.

item.title: CPU Virtualization I: Isolation & Control
item.description: Introduction In this post, we introduce resource virtualization and explore how the operating system uses this idea to give programs fair and efficient access to one of computing’s main resource: the CPU. In future posts, we’ll examine how other resources, such as RAM and Disks, are virtualized. This post is derived from the notes I made while reading the best OS book on the internet. What is Resource Virtualization? Virtualization refers to the separation of the abstract view of computing resources (compute, storage, memory) from the underlying implementations.
